name: Collado de Sa Gramola, Puerto de Andratx y Sant Elm — circular from Peguera
name_original: Collado de Sa Gramola, Puerto de Andratx y Sant Elm — circular desde Peguera
region_intro: Road cycling around Calvià offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths to challenging mountain climbs within the Serra de Tramuntana. The region features a varied coastline with cliffs and coves, alongside Mediterranean forests and serene olive groves. Cyclists can experience significant elevation changes, particularly in the mountainous areas, providing a range of experiences for different skill levels. Green spaces like the Galatzó Estate also contribute to the area's natural beauty. Best road cycling routes around Calvià The most popular road cycling route is Collado de Sa Gramola, Puerto de Andratx y Sant Elm — circular from Peguera, a 32.2 miles (51.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers coastal views and mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Western Hamlets Trail, a moderate 23.6 miles (38.0 km) path. This route winds through charming towns and offers glimpses of the region's cultural landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Coll de n'Esteve Pass – Es Grau Pass loop from es Capdellà, a 37.6 miles (60.6 km) trail leading through mountain passes and the foothills of the Tramuntana, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Calvià is defined by the Serra de Tramuntana mountains, varied coastline, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle coastal rides to demanding climbs. The routes in Calvià are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,400 reviews. More than 31,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calvià's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Calvià?
A: Calvià offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You'll find challenging mountain climbs and breathtaking panoramic views in the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Tramuntana&lt;/strong&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The region also features over 50 kilometers of varied coastline with cliffs, coves, and sandy beaches, providing stunning coastal routes. Additionally, there are tranquil areas like the Galatzó Estate and the Paseo de Calvià, offering routes through Mediterranean forests and olive groves.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Calvià?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes around Calvià listed on komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 17 easy routes, 43 moderate routes, and 20 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Calvià?
A: Yes, Calvià offers routes suitable for families. The &lt;strong&gt;Paseo de Calvià&lt;/strong&gt; is a 32-kilometer route known for its natural beauty and historical significance, ideal for a more relaxed ride. For a low-difficulty option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004003528/western-hamlets-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Western Hamlets Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 38 km long and winds through charming towns, offering a pleasant experience for families.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling climbs near Calvià?
A: Calvià is a gateway to the challenging climbs of the Serra de Tramuntana. Notable climbs in the area include &lt;strong&gt;Coll d'es Grau&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Coll de Galilea&lt;/strong&gt;. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22071827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de n'Esteve Pass – Es Grau Pass loop from es Capdellà&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gain, traversing mountain passes and the foothills of the Tramuntana.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Calvià?
A: Calvià is a prime destination for road cycling due to its generally favorable climate. While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, the region's Mediterranean climate typically offers pleasant conditions for cycling outside of the hottest summer months, making spring and autumn particularly popular for cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Calvià area?
A: Yes, many routes in Calvià are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004015201/collado-de-sa-gramola-puerto-de-andratx-y-sant-elm-circular-from-peguera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Sa Gramola, Puerto de Andratx y Sant Elm — circular from Peguera&lt;/a&gt;, which offers coastal views and mountain passes.

Q: What kind of landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Calvià offer access to various landmarks and scenic viewpoints. You can explore historic sites like &lt;strong&gt;Son Mas Castle&lt;/strong&gt; on routes such as the Llogarets de Ponent (C1) or visit historic oratories and churches like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/207645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oratory of Sant Roc in Galilea&lt;/a&gt;. Coastal routes provide stunning views of the Mediterranean Sea and Dragonera Island, while mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/444510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Galilea&lt;/a&gt; offer panoramic vistas.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Calvià?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, Calvià is well-equipped to support cyclists. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004015201/collado-de-sa-gramola-puerto-de-andratx-y-sant-elm-circular-from-peguera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Sa Gramola, Puerto de Andratx y Sant Elm — circular from Peguera&lt;/a&gt;, start from towns like Peguera, where public parking options are generally available for visitors.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes with minimal traffic in Calvià?
A: Calvià offers a mix of roads, and while some dedicated cycling lanes exist, routes that venture into the Serra de Tramuntana or through smaller hamlets like those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004003528/western-hamlets-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Western Hamlets Trail&lt;/a&gt; often provide a more tranquil cycling experience with less traffic compared to main arterial roads.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Calvià?
A: The road cycling experience in Calvià is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,400 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain passes to scenic coastal roads, and the opportunity to explore charming towns and natural beauty. Over 31,000 road cyclists have used komoot to discover the varied landscapes of Calvià.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs popular with cyclists along the routes in Calvià?
A: Calvià is a bike-friendly region, and many towns and villages along popular cycling routes, such as s'Arracó, es Capdellà, and Calvià itself, have cafes and pubs that cater to cyclists. These establishments often provide convenient stops for refreshments and rest during your ride.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that offer views of the sea?
A: Absolutely. Calvià's extensive coastline means many routes offer stunning sea views. The Fars de Ponent (C2) route, for example, combines inland roads with coastal stretches, leading to the Portopí Lighthouse and providing views of Dragonera Island. The coastal road from Palma Nova to Santa Ponsa is also a popular choice for scenic sea vistas.

Q: What is the difficulty level of road cycling routes in Calvià?
A: Calvià caters to all difficulty levels. You can find easy routes like the Llogarets de Ponent (C1) at 53 km with 690 m elevation, moderate routes such as the Fars de Ponent (C2) at 74.5 km with 1,377 m elevation, and challenging options like the Serra de Tramuntana – Sud (C4) which is 126.5 km with 2,360 m of elevation gain, including climbs through the Sóller Pass.
tips: The Coll de'n Esteve pass is very particular. The road is cut between the rocks and passes through a cleft that the locals call "heaven's gate". The asphalt is in very good condition and there is very little traffic.

Quiet mountain road in the Tramuntana Nature Park, little car traffic
A highlight both uphill and downhill!

What can I say: It's just beautiful here! The view of the bay, the small houses on the slope opposite, if you don't like that, you have a real problem...
Since the billboard is no longer there, you can enjoy the view undisturbed again.
